source: Invest Like the Best — The cyclone under the surface (EP.260)
summary: The metaverse is real and coming, but today's true metaverses are video games (Roblox, Fortnite, World of Warcraft, Destiny, Minecraft)
summary_extended: Baker argues that the metaverse is a real and meaningful long-term trend but that the term has become overhyped and misapplied — the true metaverses already exist today in the form of video games like Roblox, Fortnite, World of Warcraft, Destiny, and Minecraft, where real commerce occurs and people genuinely live digital lives. He contends that investors should look past the branding noise and recognize that gaming platforms are the foundational infrastructure of the metaverse concept. Baker is specifically excited about PlayStation VR and Apple's forthcoming headset as hardware catalysts that will accelerate the convergence of VR and AR into a unified XR paradigm. He further argues that mobile OS platform owners — Apple and Google — hold profound structural advantages in the metaverse transition because they control the software and distribution layers that any XR ecosystem will depend on. The investment implication is long-term and directional: favor gaming platforms with existing metaverse-like ecosystems (Roblox, Minecraft/Microsoft) and mobile OS gatekeepers (Apple, Google) over companies that are merely branding themselves as metaverse plays.
detail: Baker believes the metaverse trend is genuine but overhyped in branding. He identifies existing video game platforms as the real metaverses where commerce happens and people live digital lives. He is excited about PlayStation VR and Apple's upcoming headset. He believes mobile OS platforms (Apple/Android) have profound advantages in the metaverse transition as VR/AR converge into XR.
confidence_evidence: Baker expresses genuine belief but acknowledges the term has become overdone and frames his views as long-term directional rather than near-term investment calls.
quote_fragment: the only real metaverse is today...video games...real metaverses where commerce happens

ticker_audit.reasoning: Roblox is explicitly named as one of the real existing metaverses where commerce happens — it is a direct subject of this thesis.
Microsoft owns Minecraft, which Baker explicitly names as one of the true existing metaverses, making MSFT a direct subject of this thesis.

Activision Blizzard owns World of Warcraft and Destiny, both named by Baker as real metaverses, but ATVI was likely bled in from an adjacent thesis (Microsoft's acquisition of ATVI was announced days before this episode). Baker's metaverse framing focuses on the platforms themselves, not ATVI as an investment vehicle. However, given WoW and Destiny are explicitly named, ATVI has a marginal case — but as a soon-to-be-acquired company it is not an actionable standalone thesis subject. Classified tangential.
Baker explicitly calls out Apple's upcoming headset and argues mobile OS platform owners have profound structural advantages in the XR transition — AAPL is a direct beneficiary of this thesis.
Baker identifies Android/Google as the other mobile OS platform with structural advantages in the metaverse/XR transition, making GOOGL a direct beneficiary.
Baker explicitly expresses excitement about PlayStation VR, making Sony (SNE/SONY) a direct beneficiary as the hardware platform he singles out by name in the VR space.
